diarized_transcript:
[Agent] Thank you for calling Genshin Impact Player Support, this is Sam. How can I help today?
[Caller] Hi Sam. I lost my phone and I had the authenticator app on it. I can't log in now because of 2FA.
[Agent] Oh no, losing a phone is stressful on its own — and getting locked out on top of that is the worst. Don't worry, we have a clean path through this. Could I get your UID to start?
[Caller] It's 803990456.
[Agent] Thank you, Aisha. I've got the account up. Since you can't use the authenticator, we have two backup options. First, the recovery codes you saved when you enabled 2FA — ten eight-digit codes. Do you still have those saved somewhere, maybe in a password manager or printed?
[Caller] Oh, I think I saved them in my password manager. Let me check. Yes, I have them.
[Agent] Perfect. Read me one code and I'll verify it — each one works exactly once.
[Caller] OK, 48271930.
[Agent] Verified. That code is now consumed, so cross it off your list. Before I unlock the 2FA, I want to set you up properly on the new device. Do you have a new phone yet?
[Caller] Yes, I just got it yesterday.
[Agent] Great. Here's what I'll do — I'll temporarily remove the old authenticator binding, you'll log in, then immediately re-enroll 2FA on the new phone and generate a fresh set of recovery codes. The old codes expire the moment you re-enroll. Ready?
[Caller] Yes, please.
[Agent] Done. You can log in now. When you're in, go to HoYoverse Account Settings, Security, then Two-Step Verification to set it up again. I'll email you the step-by-step link.
[Caller] Got it. What about the SMS option? Is that still active?
[Agent] Good question — SMS fallback is still tied to your old number. I'd strongly recommend updating that too while you're in Security settings, so future recovery works even if the authenticator fails.
[Caller] Makes sense. Thank you so much, I thought I'd lost everything.
[Agent] Your account was never at risk, Aisha — that's what the recovery codes are for. Anything else before you go?
[Caller] No, that's it. Thanks again.
[Agent] You're very welcome. Enjoy Teyvat, and take care.
